label: lem:sumsq_is_sq
title: lem:sumsq_is_sq
statement: Let $F$ be a field in which $-1$ is not a square, and suppose every element of $F(i)$ is a square. Then sums of squares in $F$ are squares in $F$.
proof: Given $a,b\in F$, find $c,d\in F$ such that $a+bi=(c+di)^2$ in $F(i)$. Then $a=c^2-d^2$ and $b=2cd$, so $a^2+b^2=(c^2+d^2)^2$ is a square in $F$. By induction, every sum of squares in $F$ is a square in $F$.
